name: ESG Compliance Agent
note: Estimates based on Azure retail pricing. Actual costs depend on usage, region, reserved instances, and enterprise agreements.

services.name: Azure OpenAI
services.purpose: GPT-4o for ESG framework mapping, compliance gap analysis, regulatory interpretation, and report generation

services.name: Document Intelligence
services.purpose: Extract structured data from ESG reports, sustainability disclosures, audit documents, and regulatory filings

services.name: Cosmos DB
services.purpose: Compliance state, framework mappings, disclosure responses, evidence artifacts, audit history

services.name: Azure AI Search
services.purpose: Semantic search across regulatory frameworks, past disclosures, and compliance knowledge base

services.name: Container Apps
services.purpose: Compliance API — framework mapping engine, gap analysis, report generator, evidence manager

services.name: Blob Storage
services.purpose: Regulatory framework documents, evidence artifacts, generated reports, audit archives

services.name: Key Vault
services.purpose: API keys, signing certificates for reports, encryption keys for sensitive disclosures

services.name: Application Insights
services.purpose: Compliance coverage metrics, extraction accuracy, report generation latency, agent performance

optimization_tips: Use GPT-4o-mini for routine disclosure lookups and simple mappings, reserve GPT-4o for complex multi-framework gap analysis — saves 65% on AI costs
Pre-index regulatory framework text (GRI, SASB, TCFD, CSRD) in AI Search — avoids real-time document parsing for known frameworks
Cache framework-to-framework cross-mappings — GRI → SASB → TCFD mappings are static and can be cached indefinitely
Use Document Intelligence pre-built models for standard ESG report formats — custom models only for proprietary layouts
Batch compliance checks by framework — process all GRI disclosures together to maximize context window efficiency
Set Azure Cost Management budgets with alerts at 50%, 80%, 100%
